methodology_priority: If any contract or helper disagrees with strategy-rules.md, strategy-rules.md wins.

rules.summary: A stock must be down at least 60% from all-time high before investigation proceeds.
A candidate industry must be understandable before analysis proceeds.
Industries in secular decline are rejected before deeper work.
The stock must have plausible 100%+ upside over 2-3 years to stay alive.
Solvency fails when survival risk exists and price does not clearly compensate for it.
Dilution fails when SBC exceeds 5% without growth or shares are issued to service debt.
Revenue fails when there is no growth and no catalyst to change the path.
ROIC fails when returns stay chronically below threshold and the business is worst in peer group.
Base-case valuation must clear the 30% CAGR hurdle unless routed to the 20% exception human gate.
A clear multi-year margin downtrend produces PERMANENT_PASS and blocks advancement.
A weaker company may stay alive only if earlier steps passed, returns are materially higher, and alternatives are limited.
No valid catalyst means automatic pass on the stock.
Probability must be normalized to 50/60/70/80 bands before scoring and breakpoint checks.
Epistemic review must answer all five PCS questions with evidence anchors.
Confidence derives from No-count mapping first, then deterministic risk-type friction.
20-29.9% CAGR exception candidates stay in pending human review and cannot be auto-approved by the LLM pipeline.
Enrichment may demote a candidate in the final ranking but scores remain unchanged.
Final report assembly is JSON-first and markdown is rendered from the validated object.
Codex can audit methodology compliance but cannot change EdenFinTech rules or waive hard constraints.
Codex may reroute only to an earlier named stage and must state the reason code.
